comments.body:
G3 container coverage audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × this child 8937 "Producer<->consumer snapshot contract: shared schema + round-trip test through production path"), cross-checked against the full sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57).

This child is the KEYSTONE and FULLY covers:
- Parent AC1 ("A shared, single-source-of-truth snapshot-entry schema exists; both producer output and consumer reads validate against it") → 8937 AC1 (`_snapshot_schema.py` TypedDict + JSON Schema SSOT) + AC2 (fetch_snapshot validated against schema; would FAIL on pre-fix conditions).
- Parent AC2 ("One round-trip contract test exercises fetcher → snapshot → differs → applier through the production path, using fixtures derived from real Jira payloads") → 8937 AC3 (round-trip fetcher→differs→applier through production path; fails if producer key/shape diverges from a consumer read). Fixtures themselves are correctly delegated to fe57 (depends_on declared).
- Parent SC1 ("Producer and consumers can no longer drift on snapshot shape without a test failing") → directly satisfied by the joint round-trip contract; reinforced by fe57 (honest fixtures/fake) and 59ef (verified-fake honesty).
It also contributes to SC5/AC7 via 8937 AC4 (semantic assertions; no whole-blob golden equality / no mock-call-count).

All other parent criteria this child does not own are correctly delegated to named siblings: AC3/SC2 → 33d0 (collapse appliers); AC4/SC3 → 2359 (sub-op telemetry + silent-no-op canary); AC5/SC4 → 822a (hermetic bidirectional round-trip + de-encode expected-bug guards); fixtures/fake foundation → fe57; verified-fake honesty → 59ef. No per-criterion coverage gap is attributable to child 8937 itself; its declared OUT-of-scope items (fixtures→fe57, applier collapse→33d0, telemetry→2359) are consistent with the roster.

ONE ROSTER-LEVEL ABSENCE (re-cross-checked against the WHOLE roster including this child; already raised by Iso Tester on siblings 33d0, 59ef, and 822a — restated here for this pairing's record because 8937 is repeatedly named as its natural home):
- finding: Parent AC6 ("A documented anti-change-detector guardrail GOVERNS new tests — no mock-call-count, no whole-blob golden equality; assert semantic round-trip + structure/type") is only PARTIALLY covered. Every child, including this one, only APPLIES the anti-change-detector posture to its OWN tests; no sibling owns a deliverable that PRODUCES a documented governing guardrail/policy artifact that governs FUTURE/new reconciler tests.
  - criteria: parent AC6
  - location: parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 Acceptance Criteria bullet 6; cross-checked against siblings 2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57
  - evidence: child 8937 AC4 "All assertions are semantic (keys/types/round-trip), not whole-blob golden equality or interaction/mock-call-count" is self-scoped to THIS story's own tests; 2359 AC4, 33d0 AC4, 822a AC4, 59ef AC3, and fe57 are likewise self-scoped; no sibling defines/produces a documented guardrail that governs new/future tests. 8937 is repeatedly cited (by siblings 33d0/59ef/822a) as AC6's natural home, yet 8937's own ACs do not include it.
  - scenarios: a new reconciler test added next cycle has no documented guardrail to conform to; the anti-change-detector intent silently erodes once these stories close
  - impact: parent AC6 cannot be fully checked off by closing the current children as written (4-bucket: PARTIALLY covered — posture applied per-test, documented governing artifact absent)
  - checklist_item: union of children covers each parent acceptance/success criterion
  - suggested_fix: add the documented-guardrail deliverable to one story (natural home: this child 8937, the contract keystone — e.g. a new AC: "a documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests is committed and referenced by new tests") or add a small dedicated child, so AC6's documented governing artifact has an owner.

4-bucket summary for this pairing: AC1/AC2/SC1 FULLY covered by this child; SC5/AC7 contributed; AC3/AC4/AC5/SC2/SC3/SC4 covered by named siblings; AC7 structural/aggregate; AC6 PARTIALLY covered (single roster-level absence above). No coverage finding attributable to child 8937 itself for its owned criteria.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 8937 "Producer<->consumer snapshot contract: shared schema + round-trip test through production path"), cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/fe57) and corroborated against the repo. All 7 cross-child interaction modes checked under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter. VERDICT: clean pairing — no G4 finding.

Repo corroboration of 8937's premise (the schema fields it pins are real producer outputs AND consumer reads — so the SSOT is grounded, not fictional):
- fetcher.fetch_snapshot (fetcher.py L480) writes exactly the keys 8937's schema enumerates: `comment` (nested dict; L415-417, merged from get_comment_map), `issuelinks` (list; L450-454, merged from get_issuelinks_map — bug-3f04 enrichment), `parent` as `{"key": ...}` (L369, from get_parent_map). Consumers read the same: inbound_differ.py L383 `jira_fields.get("comment")`, L466 `jira_fields.get("issuelinks")`, L80 `jira_fields.get("parent")`; outbound_differ.py L969 `.get("issuelinks")`, L591 `.get("parent")`. The pre-fix conditions 8937 AC2 asserts a FAIL on (issuelinks absent; comment under wrong key) map to real historical bugs (acli_graph.py L398-402 documents the issuelinks-absent gap; comment singular-key fix at inbound_differ.py L383). The keystone's owned work is real and scoped.
- 8937's file_impact (`_snapshot_schema.py` new, `test_snapshot_contract.py` new, `fetcher.py` producer-validates-vs-schema) is DISJOINT from every sibling's edits: fe57 (tests/fixtures/jira/, _fakes.py, capture script), 59ef (test_acliclient_contract.py, acli_graph.py), 33d0 (apply_outbound.py/batch_dispatch.py/applier.py), 2359 (batch_dispatch.py/applier.py/reconcile.py), 822a (e2e_field_validation_probe.sh, test_roundtrip.py). No sibling co-edits fetcher.py or the two new files.

7-mode walk for THIS pairing:
- Implicit shared state — BENIGN. No sibling touches 8937's three files. The fetcher.py producer surface is consumed (read) by fe57's fake through fetch_snapshot, not co-edited; the schema module is net-new. No shared mutable file state.
- Conflicting assumptions — BENIGN. 8937's schema pins `comment`(nested)/`issuelinks`/`parent`/scalars; fe57 AC2 guarantees the FakeAcliClient serves fixtures through fetch_snapshot with NO shape transformation, so the fake feeds precisely the shapes 8937's schema targets — mutually reinforcing. The outbound labels list-vs-count telemetry mismatch flagged on 2359/33d0 is outbound-telemetry-internal; 8937's snapshot-entry/read-side schema neither asserts nor depends on `labels_applied` representation, so it is untouched by that hazard.
- Dependency gap — BENIGN. 8937 correctly declares depends_on fe57 (the round-trip needs the fixtures/fake). It explicitly scopes OUT fixtures (→fe57), applier collapse (→33d0), telemetry (→2359), all consistent with the roster; it needs no schema/telemetry input from any sibling to stand up the contract test. No missing input edge.
- Scope overlap — BENIGN (benign-reading filter). Four stories drive "the production path" from DISTINCT angles on DISTINCT files: 8937 = assembled snapshot-entry SCHEMA + producer→consumer key/shape round-trip (test_snapshot_contract.py); 59ef = enrichment-METHOD return-shape fidelity (test_acliclient_contract.py); 33d0 = outbound-only multi-sub-op APPLICATION correctness (apply suite); 822a = BIDIRECTIONAL inbound↔outbound behavioral ROUND-TRIP (test_roundtrip.py). Complementary, not duplicative; 8937 declares the others OUT consistently.
- Ordering violation — BENIGN. 8937's round-trip targets "the production path" by name; 33d0's end-state IS the single production applier, so 8937's test binds to the survivor by construction (33d0 edits the applier files, 8937 does not — no same-region co-edit with the 33d0↔2359 outbound-applier sequencing cluster). The only hard ordering edge (fixtures before the consuming test) is captured by depends_on fe57.
- Consumer impact — BENIGN. The new `_snapshot_schema.py` SSOT that fetcher.py validates against is additive; 8937 AC4 asserts semantic keys/types/round-trip (no whole-blob golden equality, no mock-call-count), consistent with parent AC7 "no net increase in brittle tests." No sibling is forced to conform in a way that conflicts with its own deliverable.
- Residual references — BENIGN. Every schema field references a live producer/consumer surface (verified above). 33d0 deletes the DEAD typed-leaf applier, but 8937 exercises the PRODUCTION path, so 33d0's deletion strands nothing 8937 references.

The roster-level parent-AC6 absence (documented anti-change-detector guardrail GOVERNING new tests — for which 8937 is repeatedly named the natural home) is a G3 COVERAGE matter, already tracked across the sibling set and on this ticket's prior G3 audits. It is not a G4 cross-child inconsistency, so it is not re-filed here.

VERDICT for this pairing (parent × 8937): no G4 cross-child finding; all 7 interaction modes read benign under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter.

comments.author: Iso Tester
Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS-1 review note — criterion T11 (Data-migration / backfill safety, overlay): PASS / not-applicable. No finding.

Rationale (grounded): This story adds a snapshot-entry schema and adds the `issuelinks` key (plus pins the nested `comment` / `parent` shapes) to the snapshot dict. That dict IS persisted — `.bridge_state/prev_snapshot.json` (reconcile.py L662-722), committed back between GHA runs and read at each pass start for diffing — so the T11 overlay is nominally triggered. But evaluated against T11's binary sub-checks, this persisted format is not a database-style migration:
- (a) expand-contract / no blocking DDL: N/A — single JSON file rewritten wholesale each pass from a live fetch; no table, no DDL, no lock, no downtime concept.
- (b) batching/scale: N/A — no bulk backfill transaction; the file is regenerated, not migrated in place.
- (c) resumability/idempotency: already structurally satisfied — each pass overwrites the file from a fresh fetch; a missing/old file degrades safely (reconcile.py L721-722 `prev_snapshot = {}`; "re-derive all create mutations — expensive but safe").
- (d) dual-write/in-flight handling: the one applicable angle — on the cutover pass the persisted `prev_snapshot.json` was written by OLD code (no `issuelinks`, possibly old comment shape) but read by the NEW differs/schema. The codebase handles this via defensive key-additive reads (inbound_differ `.get("issuelinks")`/`.get("comment")`/`.get("parent")`; outbound_differ `.get(...)`) and the corrupt-prev abort path (reconcile.py L666-720). The PLAN text does not narrate this, but the format makes it structurally safe — not a lost-write hazard.
- (e) rollback / data loss: store is self-regenerating (delete the file → full re-fetch, no data loss); the schema change is key-additive, so revert is safe.

Per the pass's DECISIVENESS / ANTI-FP guidance (not-applicable for non-persisted/regenerable state; do not raise an absence merely because the plan omits something the design makes safe), no T11 finding is warranted. The only nicety would be one sentence stating that a pre-change `prev_snapshot.json` (old shape, no `issuelinks`) is consumed safely on the first post-change pass — optional, since the additive-key defensive reads and the self-healing wholesale rewrite already guarantee it. Affirmations: T11 (c) resumability/idempotency and (e) rollback/no-data-loss are satisfied by the regenerable-store design.

compiled_state.description:
## Why

This is the keystone that closes the bug class. The fetcher (producer) and the differs/appliers (consumers) share an implicit contract — the snapshot-entry dict shape — that nothing pins or jointly exercises. Pin it once, and exercise producer→consumer together through the production path, so a key/shape divergence (the exact failure in bugs 0ee6 and 3f04) fails a test immediately.

## What

- A single source-of-truth **snapshot-entry schema** (TypedDict + JSON Schema) in `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/_snapshot_schema.py`, covering every field the differs read: `summary`, `description` (ADF), `status`, `priority`, `assignee`, `labels`, `comment` (nested), `issuelinks`, `parent`. Documented as the canonical shape both sides target.
- A **producer→consumer round-trip contract test** (`tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/test_snapshot_contract.py`): the `FakeAcliClient` (story A) → `fetcher.fetch_snapshot` → `inbound_differ` + `outbound_differ` → applier, through the PRODUCTION path. It asserts each consumer-depended key/shape is actually produced and read correctly (comment, issuelinks, parent + scalars). Per the epic sequencing, land it FIRST on the `comment`/`issuelinks` seam, then broaden.
- Validation asserts **structure/type, not exact values** (Dredd/Gavel posture): `fetch_snapshot` output conforms to the schema; the differs emit the expected mutations for the captured payloads. No whole-blob golden equality; no mock-call-count.
- **Owns the epic's guardrail artifact (resolves the roster-level gap):** commit a documented **anti-change-detector guardrail** for reconciler tests — a short `docs/` note (ADR-style) that records the testing-strategy decision (why one producer↔consumer contract test through the production path, rather than Pact or a live sandbox), where the snapshot schema lives, and the rules new tests must follow (assert semantic round-trip + structure/type; no mock-call-count; no whole-blob golden equality). New tests reference it.

## Scope

- In: the schema module, the joint round-trip contract test, and the documented guardrail/ADR note.
- Out: capturing fixtures (story A), collapsing appliers (story D), telemetry (story E).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `_snapshot_schema.py` defines the snapshot-entry shape (TypedDict + JSON Schema) as the single source of truth.
- [ ] A test validates `fetcher.fetch_snapshot` output against the schema, and would FAIL on the pre-fix conditions (issuelinks absent; comment under the wrong key).
- [ ] A round-trip test runs fetcher → differs → applier through the production path and asserts comment/issuelinks/parent plus scalar fields read correctly; it fails if a producer key/shape diverges from a consumer read.
- [ ] All assertions are semantic (keys/types/round-trip), never whole-blob golden equality or interaction/mock-call-count.
- [ ] A documented anti-change-detector guardrail (ADR-style note recording the testing-strategy decision + the schema location + the rules) is committed and referenced by the new tests.
